description: Baseline: unmodified s3dashmap. 25.4M ops/sec, 84.9% hit rate, CV 17.4% (noisy)

asi.hypothesis: baseline measurement
asi.note: CV=17.4% is noisy — need large improvements to be confident. ~25-28M ops/sec range across runs. 12 cores = 48 shards (default), shard_cap=625 per shard for 30K items.

description: Replace bump_freq CAS loop with simple load+store. +7.8% throughput, CV 3%

asi.hypothesis: CAS loop in bump_freq (80% of ops) replaced with relaxed load+store. Races harmless since freq is a heuristic saturating at 3.
asi.insight: fetch_update CAS loop was contended on hot entries under concurrent reads

description: Ghost set stores hashes instead of full keys — eliminates key cloning. +0.5% throughput, p99 improved 1.67→1.46µs

asi.hypothesis: Replace ghost HashSet<K> with HashSet<u64> to eliminate key cloning on eviction path
asi.result: marginal throughput gain but nice p99 improvement and code simplification
asi.insight: ghost set is only touched on write path eviction (~20% of ops), so savings are limited. The real win is less memory pressure from not cloning string keys.

description: Eliminate double map.find() in eviction + simplify freq decrement. No throughput gain (-0.75%)

asi.hypothesis: Eliminate redundant map.find() in evict_from_small (was called twice for same key) and replace CAS freq decrement with simple store under write lock
asi.rollback_reason: primary metric within noise of previous best
asi.next_action_hint: eviction path optimizations have diminishing returns on 80/20 read workload. Focus on read path or structural changes (shard count, hash function)
asi.insight: double find was already fast due to CPU cache locality — second find hits warm cache line

description: New baseline for eff_ops_sec focus. 23.1M eff ops/s, p50=0.12µs, p99=1.50µs, tail=1.38µs

asi.hypothesis: baseline with bump_freq + ghost hash optimizations already applied
asi.focus: effective throughput (ops_sec * hit_rate), p50, p99, tail latency
asi.note: hit_rate=84.9% is stable; improving it would multiply into eff_ops_sec. p99=1.5µs, tail=1.38µs are the latency targets.

description: Pre-allocate hash table + queues to shard capacity, inline hot path functions. p99: 1.50→1.46µs, tail: 1.38→1.33µs

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocate map to shard_cap, VecDeques to 2x capacity, HashSet to ghost_cap. Add #[inline] to hash_u64, determine_shard, evict_one
asi.result: consistent p99 reduction 1.50→1.46µs and tail 1.38→1.33µs across 2 runs. eff_ops modest +0.7-3.5%
asi.insight: pre-allocation eliminates resize spikes that cause tail latency. Inline hints help LTO but marginal in release profile

description: Eliminate double map.find() in eviction, replace CAS with store under write lock. +1.7% eff_ops, p99/tail maintained

asi.hypothesis: Single find() per eviction candidate instead of two; replace fetch_update CAS with simple store for freq decrement (safe under exclusive write lock)
asi.result: +1.7% eff_ops. p99=1.46 and tail=1.33 unchanged from pre-alloc improvement
asi.insight: eviction path improvements compound: pre-alloc removed resize spikes, single-find reduces per-eviction cost

description: Direct _insert bypasses Entry API: fast update-in-place for occupied, inline eviction for vacant. p99: 1.46→1.38µs, tail: 1.33→1.25µs, CV: 1.0%

asi.hypothesis: Direct _insert without Entry enum dispatch. Occupied path: single find_or_find_insert_slot + in-place value swap. Vacant path: inline ghost check + eviction + insert.
asi.result: p99 1.46→1.38µs, tail 1.33→1.25µs, CV dropped to 1.0%. eff_ops +0.4%
asi.insight: eliminating Entry enum construction + match dispatch reduces instructions on write path. The consistency improvement (CV 1.0%) suggests less variance in write latency
asi.key_win: p99 and tail latency significantly reduced

description: Reduce shard multiplier 4x→2x (64→32 shards). REGRESSION: -10% eff_ops, p99: 1.38→1.79µs

asi.hypothesis: Fewer shards (32 vs 64) = larger per-shard tables, less overhead, but more lock contention
asi.rollback_reason: significant regression in all metrics. 32 shards on 12 cores creates too much contention
asi.next_action_hint: 64 shards is correct for 12 cores. Try MORE shards or different structural changes. Do NOT reduce shard count.
asi.insight: shard_count=4*ncpu is well-tuned. contention dominates at 2*ncpu for this workload.

description: Add #[inline] to _get, _yield_read_shard, _yield_write_shard. No consistent improvement — LTO already inlines

asi.hypothesis: #[inline] on hot path trait methods to help compiler
asi.rollback_reason: no consistent improvement. first run showed good p99 but second run reverted. LTO in release profile already inlines monomorphized trait methods
asi.next_action_hint: inline hints are useless with LTO. focus on algorithmic/structural changes: reduce key cloning, improve eviction cache-friendliness, or reduce lock hold time

description: Switch ghost_set from std HashSet (SipHash) to hashbrown::HashSet (ahash). +1-2% eff_ops, p99=1.38µs maintained, CV=2.8%

asi.hypothesis: Replace std HashSet<u64> (SipHash) with hashbrown::HashSet<u64, ahash::RandomState> for ghost_set. ahash is faster for u64 keys.
asi.insight: SipHash overhead on ghost_set was measurable even though ghost set is only accessed on write path. hashbrown::HashSet also has better probe performance than std.

description: Hash-only eviction queues (VecDeque<u64> + hash in CacheEntry). +0.5% eff_ops but p99 regressed 1.38→1.42-1.46µs

asi.hypothesis: Replace VecDeque<(u64, K)> with VecDeque<u64> to eliminate key cloning. Store hash in CacheEntry for correct eviction lookup.
asi.rollback_reason: p99 regressed 1.38→1.42-1.46µs. The 8-byte hash field in CacheEntry increased per-entry size (16→24 bytes for V=Arc<String>), worsening read-path cache behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: hash-only queues need CacheEntry to stay small. Try: pack hash into existing padding, use compact hash (u32), or keep key queues but avoid String clone via Rc<str>
asi.insight: CacheEntry size matters for read latency. Each extra byte per entry = more cache misses on the 80% read path.

description: Identity hasher for ghost_set (zero-cost hash for u64). p99: 1.38→1.25µs, tail: 1.25→1.12µs, eff_ops +0.4%

asi.hypothesis: Replace ahash in ghost_set with identity hasher. Ghost set u64 values are already ahash outputs — re-hashing wastes cycles.
asi.insight: ghost_set hash overhead was measurable on write path tail latency. Identity hasher eliminates ~5ns per ghost_set operation (insert/remove/contains).
asi.key_win: both p99 and tail latency new bests

description: Increase shard count 64→128 (8x ncpu). eff_ops: 24.1→25.0M (+3.5%), p99: 1.25→1.08µs (-13.6%), tail sub-µs at 0.96µs!

asi.hypothesis: More shards (128 vs 64) = less contention with 12 threads. shard_cap=235 per shard.
asi.result: massive improvement: eff_ops +3.5%, p99 1.25→1.08µs, tail breaks sub-µs at 0.96µs!
asi.insight: 64 shards had measurable contention. 128 shards = 12 threads / 128 shards = 9.4% chance of shard collision. The reduced contention is worth the smaller per-shard tables.

description: Double ghost_cap (2× shard_cap). No hit rate improvement (still 84.9%), eff_ops -1.3%

asi.hypothesis: Larger ghost set (2× shard_cap) should catch more evicted key re-insertions, routing them to main for better retention
asi.rollback_reason: hit rate unchanged. Zipfian s=1.07 with 30K/480K is near theoretical S3-FIFO optimum (~84.9%). Ghost set at 1× already sufficient.
asi.next_action_hint: hit rate is hard to improve without algorithm changes. Try: smaller small_cap (5% vs 10%), or focus on throughput/latency/variance
asi.insight: ghost set size doesn't matter for this workload — the Zipfian skew means most re-insertions happen within 1× capacity window

description: Shrink small queue 10%→5%. Hit rate 84.9→85.0% but huge variance (CV 10.5%), eff_ops unstable

asi.hypothesis: Smaller small queue (5% vs 10%) gives more main slots for hot entries, improving hit rate
asi.rollback_reason: variance too high — eff_ops swung 23-25M across 2 runs, CV 10.5%. User requires robustness.
asi.next_action_hint: 5% small is too aggressive. Try 7-8% or adjust MAX_FREQ instead. Hit rate confirmed improvable by tuning small/main ratio.
asi.insight: hit rate 85.0% confirmed across 2 runs (vs 84.9% baseline). Small 5% catches promotions faster but makes eviction pattern more sensitive to op ordering.

description: Shrink small queue 10%→7%. Hit rate unchanged, no clear improvement on any target

asi.hypothesis: 7% small / 93% main might improve hit rate while being more stable than 5%
asi.rollback_reason: hit rate unchanged at 84.9%, eff_ops and latency similar to previous keep, no improvement on any target
asi.next_action_hint: small/main ratio tuning at 7-10% doesn't affect this Zipfian workload. The 5% did get 85.0% but with bad variance. Focus on throughput/latency instead.
asi.insight: at shard_cap=235, small_cap changes from 24 to 17 entries — too small a difference to affect eviction quality

description: 256 shards (16×ncpu). Best latency ever (p99=0.96-1.00µs) but CV consistently 10-12% — too variable

asi.hypothesis: 256 shards (16×ncpu) reduces contention further. shard_cap=118.
asi.rollback_reason: CV consistently 10-12% across 2 runs. User requires robustness. 256 shards with 118 entries/shard makes per-iteration performance sensitive to hash distribution.
asi.next_action_hint: 128 shards (8×ncpu) is the sweet spot for this workload. Diminishing returns on shard count. Focus on other dimensions.
asi.insight: shard count vs robustness tradeoff: more shards = better latency but smaller per-shard tables = more variance. 128 is the balance point.

description: Pre-clone key before write lock to reduce lock hold time. Marginal — wasted clone for 85% occupied writes offsets benefit

asi.hypothesis: Pre-clone key before write lock moves allocation out of critical section
asi.rollback_reason: no consistent improvement across 2 runs. 85% of writes are occupied (clone wasted), only 3% of total ops benefit from faster lock hold time.
asi.next_action_hint: key cloning is not the bottleneck. Only 3% of ops do vacant inserts. Focus on read path (80% of ops) or reduce variance.
asi.insight: speculative pre-clone hurts more than it helps — extra alloc+dealloc overhead on 17% of ops (occupied writes) is measurable

description: 3-run median benchmark for stability. Baseline: eff_ops=24.7M, p99=1.25µs, tail=1.12µs, CV=3.4%

asi.hypothesis: 3-run median benchmark reduces measurement noise. Same code as before.
asi.result: stable measurement: 24.66M median of [25.0M, 24.0M, 24.66M]. Previous single-run results ranged 24-25M.
asi.insight: single benchmark runs have ~5-10% noise. 3-run median gives a reliable signal for comparing changes.

description: Fix flaky tests (min per-shard cap=8 for small caches) + bump freq on occupied inserts. Hit rate 84.9→85.2%, all tests pass 20/20

asi.hypothesis: Two changes: (1) auto-reduce shard count for small caches so shard_cap >= 8 (fixes birthday-paradox eviction in tests), (2) bump_freq on occupied inserts so writes count as accesses for eviction decisions
asi.result: hit rate 84.9→85.2% confirmed across 2 sessions. All tests pass 20/20 runs (was flaky before). Tight benchmark clustering [24.6, 24.6, 24.8M].
asi.insight: the 0.3% hit rate improvement comes from treating writes as accesses — frequently-written keys survive eviction better. The shard fix is pure correctness.
asi.key_wins: hit rate +0.3%, flaky test fix, robust measurements

description: S3-FIFO paper: reset freq→0 on second chance (was decrement by 1). eff_ops +1.2%, CV -40%, but hit rate 85.2→84.9% (regression)

asi.hypothesis: S3-FIFO paper resets freq to 0 on second chance instead of decrementing. Faster eviction, more predictable.
asi.rollback_reason: hit rate dropped 85.2→84.9%. User requires higher hit rate. The faster eviction evicts entries that would have survived with freq-decrement.
asi.next_action_hint: keep the gradual freq decrement — it gives entries more chances which helps hit rate. The bump_freq on occupied writes is the better hit rate optimization.
asi.insight: freq reset vs decrement: reset gives better throughput (+1.2%), latency (-3.6% p99), and remarkable consistency [24.9, 24.9, 25.0M] but costs 0.3% hit rate. Tight tradeoff.
asi.notable: 3 runs within 0.2% of each other — most consistent measurement ever

description: Increase MAX_FREQ 3→7: more eviction resistance. eff_ops 24.6→25.2M (+2.4%), hit rate 85.2% maintained, p99=1.21µs

asi.hypothesis: MAX_FREQ 7 gives entries more second chances in main queue, improving eviction quality and hit rate retention.
asi.result: eff_ops 25.2M (new best), hit_rate 85.2% maintained, benchmark runs [25.6, 25.2, 24.8M]
asi.insight: higher MAX_FREQ means hot entries accumulate more freq over time and survive more eviction passes. With bump_freq on writes too, frequently-written keys get better protection. The throughput gain comes from fewer actual evictions (more second chances = more entries stay in cache).
asi.key_wins: new eff_ops best at 25.2M while maintaining 85.2% hit rate

description: MAX_FREQ 15: too many second chances, eviction gets expensive. eff_ops 25.2→24.6M (-2.4%)

asi.hypothesis: MAX_FREQ=15 gives even more eviction resistance
asi.rollback_reason: eff_ops dropped 2.4%. Too many second chances means evict_from_main does 15 re-enqueues per cold entry, making eviction O(15) per slot instead of O(7)
asi.next_action_hint: MAX_FREQ=7 is the sweet spot. Don't go higher. Try MAX_FREQ=5 to see if lower is better.
asi.insight: eviction cost = MAX_FREQ * (pop_front + find + push_back) per cold entry in main. At 15 this dominates insert latency.

description: MAX_FREQ=5: huge variance [21.5M, 25.6M, 23.9M], eff_ops -5.3%. MAX_FREQ=7 confirmed optimal

asi.hypothesis: MAX_FREQ=5 might be a better balance between eviction cost and retention
asi.rollback_reason: massive variance across 3 runs (21.5-25.6M). eff_ops median 23.9M vs 25.2M at MAX_FREQ=7.
asi.insight: MAX_FREQ=5 gives fewer second chances, making eviction patterns more sensitive to key ordering within each iteration

description: Shard routing: mask (low bits) instead of double-shift. eff_ops -1.2%, p99 regressed 1.21→1.38µs

asi.hypothesis: Use hash & mask (1 AND) instead of (hash << 7) >> shift (2 shifts) for shard selection. Low bits are independent from hashbrown's high-7-bit SIMD tag.
asi.rollback_reason: eff_ops down 1.2%, p99 regressed significantly 1.21→1.38µs. Low bits may correlate with hashbrown's internal H1 probe hash, worsening intra-shard distribution.
asi.next_action_hint: the original double-shift shard routing is well-designed. Don't change it.
asi.insight: shard routing matters more than expected. The bits used for shard selection must be independent from hashbrown's full probe sequence, not just the SIMD tag.

description: find() fast path for occupied inserts (85% of writes). eff_ops 25.2→25.5M (+1.1%), hit rate 85.2%, remarkably stable across 6 runs

asi.hypothesis: Use simpler find() for 85% occupied writes, fall back to find_or_find_insert_slot only for 15% vacant. find() skips insert-slot tracking.
asi.result: eff_ops stable at 25.4-25.5M across 2 sessions (6 benchmark runs). Previous best was 25.2M.
asi.insight: find() is cheaper than find_or_find_insert_slot for found case because it doesn't track empty/deleted slots during probing. 85% of writes benefit from this.

description: Verification run — no code change. eff_ops 24.7M, p99=1.00µs, hit=85.2%

asi.hypothesis: verification run, no changes
asi.rollback_reason: no change to evaluate
asi.next_action_hint: try hash fingerprint in CacheEntry padding + hash-only queues

description: Hash-only eviction queues with u16 fingerprint in CacheEntry padding (0-byte cost). Eliminates key cloning. eff_ops 25.5→25.8-26.3M (+1-3%), hit 85.2%

asi.hypothesis: Store u16 hash fingerprint in CacheEntry padding (no size increase), switch queues from VecDeque<(u64, K)> to VecDeque<u64>. Eviction uses hash_check + loc matching instead of key comparison.
asi.result: eff_ops 25.8-26.3M (up from 25.5M). Key clone eliminated on vacant inserts. CacheEntry stays 16 bytes.
asi.insight: the u16 fingerprint + loc check gives <0.1% false positive rate per shard. Queue entries shrink from 32 bytes to 8 bytes (4x smaller). Memory savings: ~1.4MB across 128 shards.
asi.key_wins: new eff_ops record 26.3M, zero-cost fingerprint via padding reuse

description: u32 fingerprint instead of u16. Slightly worse eff_ops (25.3 vs 25.8M), higher CV (16.3%)

asi.hypothesis: u32 fingerprint reduces collision risk further
asi.rollback_reason: eff_ops down, CV higher. The u16 comparison was faster or the extra 2 bytes affect codegen subtly.
asi.next_action_hint: u16 fingerprint is optimal. Don't increase.

description: 5-run median benchmark for max stability. eff_ops=26.1M (+12.9% from baseline), CV=2.1%, hit=85.2%, p99=1.25µs

asi.hypothesis: 5-run median reduces outlier impact for more stable measurements
asi.insight: 5 runs eliminate single-run thermal/scheduling noise. The true steady-state performance is ~26M eff_ops.

description: unreachable_unchecked for dead Ok arm in _insert. No improvement (25.8 vs 26.1M), risky UB

asi.hypothesis: unreachable_unchecked eliminates dead branch codegen in vacant fallback
asi.rollback_reason: no measurable improvement. 25.8M vs 26.1M is within noise. UB risk not worth it.
asi.next_action_hint: compiler already optimizes dead branches well with LTO. Low-level hint micro-optimizations are exhausted.

description: Direct insert() instead of find_or_find_insert_slot for vacant path. No improvement (26.0 vs 26.1M)

asi.hypothesis: Use RawTable::insert() directly instead of find_or_find_insert_slot + insert_in_slot for vacant path. Avoids second probe.
asi.rollback_reason: no measurable improvement. 26.0M vs 26.1M is within noise. hashbrown's find_or_find_insert_slot is already efficient.
asi.insight: the second probe for vacant inserts (15% of writes = 3% of ops) is too rare to meaningfully impact overall throughput

description: Final verification — no code change. Confirms steady state: 26.1M eff_ops, p99=1.17µs, hit=85.2%

asi.hypothesis: verification run
asi.rollback_reason: no code change
asi.summary: optimization is mature. 14 optimizations kept across 2 sessions. +12.9% eff_ops, +0.3% hit rate, -17% p99, -19% tail from baseline. Remaining gains require algorithmic changes (different eviction policy, different lock design) or hardware-level tuning.

description: Set freq=MAX_FREQ on promotion to main. Hit rate regressed 85.2→85.0%, eff_ops -2.7%

asi.hypothesis: Reset freq to MAX_FREQ on small→main promotion for maximum eviction resistance
asi.rollback_reason: hit rate dropped 0.2%, eff_ops -2.7%. Over-protection of barely-accessed promoted entries (freq=1→7) blocks eviction of cold entries, worsening overall cache quality.
asi.next_action_hint: promotion should PRESERVE freq, not inflate it. The current behavior (keep freq as-is) is correct — entries earn their protection through actual accesses.
asi.insight: p99=1.04µs and tail=0.92µs were excellent because fewer evictions completed, but at the cost of cache quality

description: Early drop of write lock in occupied insert + fix median script bug. Script was selecting wrong run as median

asi.hypothesis: Explicit drop(shard) before return to release write lock sooner
asi.rollback_reason: found script bug — median lookup only searched first 3 of 5 runs. Previous results may have been wrong. Also early drop showed no improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: rerun clean baseline with fixed script before any new experiments
asi.script_bug: median line lookup used 'for i in 0 1 2' instead of 'for i in 0 1 2 3 4' — if median value was from run 4 or 5, wrong line was reported
